name: Synthetic Data Generator
tagline: Build training datasets from natural-language descriptions
seoTitle: Synthetic Data Generator - Build Datasets in NL
metaDescription: Synthetic Data Generator is an Argilla tool that builds LLM training datasets from natural-language descriptions using distilabel and your choice of LLM.
overview: Synthetic Data Generator is a tool from Argilla for creating datasets used to train and fine-tune language models. You describe the kind of data you want in plain language, and it uses distilabel and an LLM to generate matching examples. It runs as a Gradio app you can launch locally or on Hugging Face Spaces.

It targets ML engineers and researchers who need custom datasets but do not want to label everything by hand. You can iterate on a small sample first, then scale up to a full dataset, and push the result to the Hugging Face Hub or to Argilla for review and curation.

Within the synthetic data and labeling space, it focuses on three task types: text classification, chat data for supervised fine-tuning, and retrieval-augmented generation. Because each pipeline is built on distilabel, you can swap the LLM or adjust the pipeline steps. Note that the original authors have moved on and now point users to a successor project, aisheets.
features: Describe the dataset you want in natural language and generate samples to iterate on before producing the full-scale dataset
Supports text classification, chat data for supervised fine-tuning, and retrieval-augmented generation tasks
Built on distilabel, so you can change the LLM or customize the pipeline steps
Works with multiple providers via environment variables: Hugging Face Inference, OpenAI-compatible APIs, Ollama, and vLLM
Push generated datasets to the Hugging Face Hub or to Argilla for curation, or save them to a local directory
Tunable generation through env vars like MAX_NUM_ROWS, MAX_NUM_TOKENS, and DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, set a Hugging Face token, and launch the Gradio app to start generating datasets.

steps.heading: Set your Hugging Face token
steps.body: Set HF_TOKEN to push datasets to the Hub and use free completions from Hugging Face Inference Endpoints.

steps.heading: Launch the app
steps.body: Call launch() to start the Gradio interface, then describe the dataset you want.

steps.heading: Choose a different model or provider (optional)
steps.body: Override the model and base URL with env vars to use OpenAI-compatible APIs, Ollama, or vLLM instead of the default.

useCases: Bootstrap a text-classification dataset for a new domain when you have no labeled data yet
Generate chat conversations for supervised fine-tuning of an instruction-following model
Create question-answer pairs to build or evaluate a retrieval-augmented generation system
Prototype a sample dataset quickly, then scale it up and push it to the Hugging Face Hub or Argilla for review
